mode_selected_reason: FUSE mismatch on 5 git-owned files at dispatcher quick-check. data/{wins,sections,uncounted-failures,predictions}.json and docs/index.html were all 3 commits behind git main. Disk also at 82% (WARNING threshold crossed, though stable). Previous report flagged infra/schedule anomalies.

pipeline_health.details: All scheduled agents producing on cadence: poller 04:14Z, decider 08:18Z (ran 4 times today), curmudgeon 08:25Z, analyst 06:18Z, integrity 07:07Z, workspace-sync 06:03Z. tracker.json fresh (0h). 38 open issues, 1016 closed. Curmudgeon queue depth 6 (modest). 71 WINs (from 69 baseline).

infrastructure.details: 5 git-owned files STALE on FUSE workspace vs GitHub main: data/wins.json, data/sections.json, data/uncounted-failures.json, data/predictions.json, docs/index.html. Workspace was frozen at commit 729ad70 (Decider 2026-04-20T04:00 self-apply). Git main is at 662d3b4 — three newer decider commits (026ce27, 2d663df, 662d3b4) had not propagated to FUSE because decider does not invoke `build.js publish` on self-apply commits, and PROP-010 (reverse git→workspace sync) remains unwritten. Self-fixed via direct cp of all 5 files from fresh clone to workspace; post-fix md5 matches GitHub raw. Auth OK (HTTP 200, 93-char PAT). Disk 82% (1803MB free) — flat at 82-83% for 8 days, hair over WARNING threshold but trend stable.

proposals_and_fixes.details: One conservative self-fix applied (FUSE sync). PROP-010 still owed and is the root cause of today's Mode 2 trigger — belongs in a dedicated Mode 4 run.

dome_social_missed_2026-04-19.prior_claim: dome-social did not fire on 2026-04-19; nextRunAt jumped to 2026-04-20
dome_social_missed_2026-04-19.actual: Prior report was OBSERVATIONALLY WRONG. dome-social did fire at 2026-04-19T11:34Z — just six minutes after the prior tinker (which ran at 11:28Z) observed its nextRunAt. The apparent skip was jitter between tinker's observation and social's actual execution. No action needed. Social next runs 2026-04-20T09:05Z (~20 min from this report).

dome_workspace_sync_skipped_runs.actual: Fully recovered. workspace-sync has run 3 times in the last 24h: 2026-04-19T11:43Z, 2026-04-20T04:26Z, 2026-04-20T06:03Z. Next due 2026-04-20T10:03Z. Schedule fully restored to `0 */4 * * *` cadence with no further misses.

PROP_010_reverse_sync.status: STILL UNWRITTEN — today's Mode 2 trigger is direct evidence this is needed. 3 decider commits in 4 hours produced 5 stale FUSE files. Each decider run widens the gap. Owed for 3+ tinker cycles now.

status_json_schedule_note_stale.prior_claim: status.json says 'Curmudgeon interval is 30m (fast)' — actual is hourly
status_json_schedule_note_stale.actual: Still stale. status.json (written 2026-04-20T04:14Z by poller, not refreshed by subsequent decider runs) reads 'ATTENTION: Curmudgeon interval is 30m (fast) but mode is BAU. Human should restore to 240m.' Actual cron for dome-curmudgeon is `20 * * * *` (hourly at :20), not 30m. Note is two adjustments stale: curmudgeon went 30m → hourly, but status.json still says 30m.

self_fixes_applied_this_run.detail: cp from /tmp/dmr-check (clean clone at main HEAD 662d3b4) to workspace: data/wins.json, data/sections.json, data/uncounted-failures.json, data/predictions.json, docs/index.html. Post-fix all 5 md5 hashes match GitHub raw. No content mutation; strictly sync git→workspace (the missing half of build.js publish's workspace sync step). Idempotent, zero-risk.
self_fixes_applied_this_run.justification: Mode 2 procedure Step 1 explicitly permits: 'Fix: copy from clone or GitHub raw fetch to workspace.' Integrity runs against workspace docs/index.html; today it ran at 07:07Z against stale FUSE but top-line counts happen to have been invariant (71 WINs at all four relevant commits). Future integrity runs are now protected until the next decider self-apply.

error_pattern_scan.hard_errors_last_7d: NONE — no 'command not found', 'Authentication failed', 'Operation not permitted', 'ENOENT', 'push fail' matches in recent daily-reports or social/analysis/tinker reports.
error_pattern_scan.noteworthy: Decider 07:00Z report itself flags `FUSE expansion-tracker is stale` — decider is aware of the staleness class and self-reads from its clone. This is defensive design working as intended (decider is freshness-protected), but it means the tinker-observed FUSE drift is systemic, not incidental.

findings.finding: FUSE workspace was 3 decider commits stale for git-owned files (wins.json, sections.json, uncounted-failures.json, predictions.json, docs/index.html)
findings.root_cause: Decider self-apply commits land git-owned files via a clean clone then `git push`. `build.js publish` is the only mechanism that syncs git→FUSE. Decider does not invoke publish on every self-apply run, so the workspace grows stale between publishes. PROP-010 (reverse git→workspace sync) would close this gap but has never been written.
findings.self_fix_applied: yes — cp git-clone → workspace for all 5 files this run
findings.followup: Write PROP-010 in next Mode 4 run

findings.finding: monitor/status.json schedule_note is two adjustments stale (says 'Curmudgeon interval is 30m' — actual cron is `20 * * * *` = hourly)
findings.root_cause: Decider writes status.json each run but appears not to be refreshing schedule_note when the interval changes. Prior tinker report flagged this; one cycle later, still stale.
findings.self_fix_applied: no — workspace-owned live-state file, decider territory
findings.followup: Decider should refresh on next run (or human op note)

findings.finding: Disk at 82%, flat for 8 days, one crashed clone from full
findings.root_cause: Clone accumulation from churn-and-burn cadence. Clones delete correctly in the normal path; the risk is a crashed agent between clone and cleanup.
findings.followup: Tracked; PROP-010 partly addresses (if FUSE can serve as truth for git-owned reads, fewer clones needed); also consider a nightly orphan-clone reaper.

findings.finding: Prior report's dome-social anomaly was a false positive — social did run 2026-04-19T11:34Z, six minutes after prior tinker observed its state
findings.root_cause: Prior tinker inferred 'missed today' from nextRunAt without accounting for observation-vs-execution race. Self-audit lesson.
findings.followup: Future tinker schedule-gap checks should cross-reference lastRunAt timestamp against claimed 'skip' and flag if observation-to-nextRunAt is <1h (within possible jitter).

recommendations.recommendation: Write PROP-010 — reverse git→workspace sync. This is today's Mode 2 trigger and has been owed for 3+ cycles. Architectural debt compounds every decider run.
PROP-005 — 16 consecutive no-op proposals. Close as wontfix or re-scope. Reiterated from prior report.
PROP-008 gates — 5-day-old unimplemented. Decide to implement or close.
Refresh status.json schedule_note on next run. Current text is two adjustments stale (still says 30m curmudgeon interval; actual cron is hourly).
Re-verify PROP-009r2 flip criteria — day 2 of 7 consecutive clean days. Track bypass_tuples_per_day, strict_queue_id pops.

counter_arguments.claim: Manually cp-ing 5 files from clone to FUSE masks the real problem; better to leave it broken and force the PROP-010 fix.
counter_arguments.counter: Mode 2 procedure explicitly sanctions this fix ('copy from clone or GitHub raw fetch to workspace'). Integrity runs against workspace docs/index.html; leaving it stale risks integrity reporting against pre-patch data. Also: the self-fix is idempotent, zero-content-mutation, takes 2 seconds. The PROP-010 recommendation is still loud and clear in this report. The fix does not compete with the PROP — both are needed.

counter_arguments.claim: Prior report's dome-social 'missed run' was a real observation; re-interpreting it as a false positive is convenient revisionism.
counter_arguments.counter: Evidence is concrete: task list shows lastRunAt=2026-04-19T11:34Z, which is six minutes AFTER the prior tinker ran. A six-minute gap between observation and actual execution is well within scheduler jitter. The skip-inference was based on nextRunAt alone; cross-checking lastRunAt would have caught the false positive. This is a lesson for future runs, not revisionism.

counter_arguments.claim: FUSE drift is business-as-usual architectural cost; every tinker run noting it adds noise without progress.
counter_arguments.counter: Three reasons this is wrong. (1) The drift has accelerated — churn-and-burn decider rate (2h cycle, multiple commits per cycle) means more drift more often. (2) Integrity is freshness-UN-protected and runs daily against workspace HTML; stale HTML risks stale anchor/link checks. (3) PROP-010 is a bounded, specific piece of work — not an open-ended recommendation. Every run without the PROP being written is a day closer to a real failure mode, not a routine observation.

counter_arguments.claim: Running Mode 2 when the prior run was also Mode 3 then Mode 2 suggests dispatcher oscillation; perhaps the mode-selection logic is too sensitive to FUSE state.
counter_arguments.counter: Dispatcher selected Mode 2 correctly per its published rules (any STALE file → Mode 2). The oscillation is a symptom of PROP-010 not being written — fix that, and Mode 2 rarely triggers. Weakening the dispatcher instead would hide the real problem.
